title: Earnings Manipulation: A firm has an EBIT of $300,000, interest expenses of...
summary: Calculate net income from EBIT by subtracting interest expense and applying the tax rate.
prompt: A firm has an EBIT of $300,000, interest expenses of $50,000, and tax rate of 30%. Calculate the net income before extraordinary items.
solution: Net Income = (EBIT - Interest Expense) × (1 - Tax Rate) = ($300,000 - $50,000) × (1 - 0.30) = $250,000 × 0.70 = $175,000. The net income before extraordinary items is a critical metric for assessing a company's profitability.
